name: Neil Buys - Nevada Coin Mart - Jones & Flamingo - One Location Only
description_short: Nevada Coin Mart buys and sells jewelry, coins, watches, diamonds, and collectibles at a single Las Vegas location. They offer free appraisals and same-day cash for precious metals and valuables.
description_long:
Nevada Coin Mart, operating as 'Neil Buys,' has been in business since 1949 and claims to have served millions of customers while paying out millions in cash. The company operates a single physical location at 4065 S. Jones Boulevard in Las Vegas, Nevada, directly across from the post office near an Arco station. This long operational history positions them as an established player in the precious metals and collectibles buying market in the Las Vegas area.

The company specializes in purchasing a wide range of items including all types of jewelry (gold, silver, vintage, estate, costume, designer brands), watches (Rolex, Breitling, Cartier, Chopard, Tag Heuer, Omega), coins (US, foreign, graded, Morgan dollars, 90% silver coins), diamonds (loose or set, certified or uncertified, engagement rings), currency (US and foreign bills, including rare $500 and $1000 denominations, Confederate currency), bullion, and collectibles (statues, figurines, vases, artwork, playing cards, comics). They also operate a retail "For Sale" section offering bullion, coins, currency, and silver poured art.

Nevada Coin Mart distinguishes itself through several operational features: they operate 365 days per year from 9am to 6pm, accept walk-ins, offer 24/7 phone/text contact, provide free price quotes within 10 minutes or less, and conduct on-site testing using x-ray spectrometer equipment visible to customers. Their single-location-only model contrasts with chain pawn shops, and their specialization in precious metals and collectibles rather than general pawn lending positions them differently from traditional pawn operations.

As a business model, Nevada Coin Mart functions as a precious metals and collectibles buyer rather than a traditional pawn lender offering collateral-based loans. While categorized correctly as a pawn shop (which encompasses gold/jewelry buyers and collateral-based operations), consumers should understand they are selling items for cash, not borrowing against collateral. The company's emphasis on buying rather than lending, their single location, and their focus on precious metals make them most suitable for those looking to liquidate specific valuable items rather than those seeking short-term loans.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: In-person testing using x-ray spectrometer equipment conducted transparently in front of customers
Open 365 days per year, 9am-6pm, with 24/7 phone/text availability for inquiries
Free price quotes delivered within 10 minutes or less via online request
Established business since 1949 with stated track record of serving millions of customers
Single location focus may indicate specialized expertise and consistent management
Walk-in friendly with accessible location across from post office on Jones Boulevard
Extensive buying categories across jewelry, coins, watches, diamonds, currency, and collectibles
cons: Single location only limits accessibility for consumers outside Las Vegas area
No transparent pricing information or buying rates published on website
Limited independent verification of claimed historical payouts and customer base
No published credentials, certifications, or third-party accreditations listed beyond general claims
No information about loan offerings if customer needs collateral-based lending rather than outright sale
best_for: Las Vegas and Henderson residents looking to sell jewelry, coins, or watches for immediate cash
Collectors of precious metals, rare coins, or currency seeking specialized buyers
Individuals liquidating estate jewelry or vintage collectibles
diagnosis: Nevada Coin Mart is best suited for Las Vegas-area residents who own jewelry, coins, watches, or precious metals they want to sell for immediate cash, not for those seeking collateral-based loans. The primary caveat is that this is a buy/sell operation, not a traditional pawn lender, and they operate from a single location, making them inaccessible to consumers outside the Las Vegas valley.
services: Gold jewelry buying (all types including scrap, broken, chains, bracelets)
Silver jewelry buying (sterling flatware, turquoise, silver-plated items)
Designer jewelry purchasing (Cartier, Chopard, Buccellati, Scott Kay, Georg Jensen, Georg Jensen)
Luxury watch buying (Rolex, Breitling, Cartier, Tiffany, Omega, Tag Heuer, Chopard)
Coin buying and trading (US coins, foreign coins, graded/slabbed coins, Morgan dollars, 90% silver coins, American Platinum Eagles)
Diamond purchasing (loose, set, certified, uncertified, all cuts/colors/clarities, engagement rings)
Precious metals bullion buying (bars, coins, rounds)
Currency buying (US and foreign bills, $500-$1000 bills, notes, certificates, Confederate currency)
Rare collectibles purchasing (statues, figurines, vases, artwork, playing cards, comics)
Free in-person jewelry appraisals and x-ray spectrometer testing
Retail sales of bullion, coins, currency, and silver poured art
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
